source: Invest Like the Best — AI, semiconductors, and the robotic frontier (EP.385)
summary: Model Flops Utilization (MFU) decomposed into MAMF × SFU × checkpoint frequency × PUE is the key efficiency equation determining which AI labs win
summary_extended: Baker presents a unified analytical framework he calls the MFU (Model Flops Utilization) equation, decomposed as MFU = MAMF (model-aware memory footprint, capturing software and CUDA efficiency per GPU) × SFU (system flops utilization, capturing networking, storage, and memory bandwidth efficiency) × (1 - checkpoint frequency) × PUE (power utilization efficiency). He argues that AI labs with superior scores across all four components can achieve a 100%+ advantage in exaflops per capex dollar per watt of electricity, which directly determines model quality, time-to-market speed, and inference cost competitiveness. Baker developed this framework the morning of the interview and presents it as his primary lens for evaluating which AI labs will win the scaling race. Critically, he argues the equation also maps directly to investment opportunities: each inefficient layer (networking, storage, memory, power) represents a product category where vendors who improve SFU or PUE capture outsized value. No specific timeframe is given, but the implication is medium-term as labs compete on infrastructure efficiency during the current scaling buildout.
detail: Baker proposes a unified AI efficiency equation: MFU = MAMF (software/CUDA efficiency per GPU) × SFU (system flops efficiency capturing networking, storage, memory) × (1 - checkpoint frequency) × PUE (power utilization efficiency). Labs with superior scores on this equation can achieve 100%+ advantage in exaflops per capex dollar per watt, determining model quality, time-to-market, and inference cost. This equation also maps directly to investment opportunities at each inefficient layer.
confidence_evidence: Baker explicitly states this is his primary analytical framework for evaluating labs and identifying investment opportunities; developed the morning of the interview
quote_fragment: exoflops per second per dollar of capex per watt of electricity
